bylaw: City of Yellowknife Building By-law No. 5058

6.title: EnerGuide Rating System Technical Procedures v15 adopted

7.title: NBC modified for the City as set out in this By-law

8.title: Reference to By-law includes Parts 1, 2, and Schedules

9.title: By-law applies to any work prescribed in the N.B.C.

10.title: By-law purpose does not extend to economic loss, warranty, defect

13.title: Council resource allocation to Building Services Division

14.title: By-law administered and enforced by Building Services Division

15.title: Paramountcy — By-law prevails where it exceeds the N.B.C.

17.title: Exemptions — infrastructure, field office, <10m² accessory, landscaping <600mm, decks/retaining <600mm, minor mechanical

18.title: By-law does not apply to applications received before effective date

19.title: Permit required — offence to commence work before permit issued

20.title: Permit issuance does not relieve applicant of responsibility / constitute warranty

21.title: Prohibited acts — tampering, variance without approval, false information, contravention, obstruction

22.title: Permit issuance — refusal / issue / conditions / partial / restore site / expiry (2 years) / extension / revocation

23.title: Permit Applications — online, legal description, site plans, RDP requirements, demolition, revisions, abandoned (180 days)

24.title: Occupancy — certificate required, issuance criteria (letters of assurance, life safety tested), partial OC, incomplete with deposit

25.title: Notification — applicant requests inspections after each stage

26.title: Inspection and inspection report — within 2 business days

28.title: Posting — no alteration or removal of posted permit/notice/order

29.title: Applicant responsibilities — sole responsibility, document availability on site, no unsafe condition, no concealment before inspection, occupancy only with OC, Real Property Report

30.title: RDP (Registered Design Professional) — Inspector may require for specialized work; letters of assurance; field reviews

31.title: Inspectors — powers including written directions, corrections, entry (subject to §37)

32.title: Qualifications — Class 1 (1-2 unit dwelling), Class 2 (Part 9), Class 3 (all NBC)

34.title: Exclusions of Liability — no proceeding against Manager or Inspector for good-faith acts

35.title: City — may demolish / remove / make safe at applicant expense; may take action without notice in imminent danger

36.title: Enforcement — Inspector may enter, require documents, conduct tests, require samples at applicant expense

37.title: Entry of the Work — Inspector may not enter occupied part except with consent / exigent circumstances / 24-hr notice / imminent danger

38.title: Orders — Uncover Work, Stop-Work, Unsafe Condition, Do Not Occupy, Removal of Unauthorized Encroachment, Removal of Structure

39.title: Content of Orders — on prescribed form

42.title: Effect of Order — no contravention except work authorized by Inspector in writing

43.title: Responsibility for Cost of Complying with an Order — applicant bears costs

44.title: Review of Order — Council review on written request within 14 days; heard within 21 days

45.title: Offences and Penalties — Summary Conviction up to $2,000 individual / $10,000 corporation / 6 months imprisonment

46.title: General Offences — false information, failure to comply, contravention; strict liability; ticket; per-day continuing offence; joint/several liability

47.title: Permit Fees — per Fees and Charges By-law, paid before permit issuance

48.title: Construction Value — Inspector may use market value to validate declared value

49.title: Refund — exclude admin fee; one-half if permit issued and no work; no refund if work begun or inspection completed

50.title: Re-Inspection Fee — charge may apply if work fails inspection or not ready when requested

51.title: Double fees — permit fees may be doubled if work occurs before permit issued

52.title: Schedules — A Offences & Penalties, B Concrete Piers, C Energy P9 Residential, D Energy P3 Residential, E Retaining Walls

53.title: Part 2 Application — local construction requirements in addition to N.B.C.

54.title: Continuous concrete foundation pinned to bedrock — Part 9 without RDP; rebar / dowel / wall-height specs (max 3.66 m)

55.title: Concrete foundations — all other aspects conform to by-law

56.title: Concrete pier foundation pinned to bedrock — Part 9 without RDP if conforming to Schedule B

57.title: Surface Mount Foundation — woodblocks / concrete pads / concrete slab ≥100mm for non-residential accessory <55 m²; concrete specs

58.title: All other foundation types — designed per NBC Part 4 by RDP with letters of assurance

59.title: Standpipe System — per NFPA 14; 65mm SIPT fire dept connection; RDP-designed with stamped drawings

60.title: Sprinkler System — CSSD / CWBSP / RDP may design Part 9; RDP required for Part 3; NFPA + NBC compliance

61.title: Multi-Family Dwelling — ≥3 units must have sprinkler per NFPA 13

62.title: Hydrants — 4-inch and two 2½-inch National Standard Thread; compatible with YK Fire Department

63.title: Wall hydrants — not considered a water source for distance-to-FDC calculations

64.title: Energy Efficiency — NBC P9 res RSI values (Table 1), FDWR 15%; Performance Path (Table 2 TEDI 105 kWh/m²a, -25% vs ref); P9 non-res per NBC; P3 res per Schedule D; P3 non-res per NECB

65.title: Mechanical System — Inspector may require RDP for Part 9 mechanical if not designed per NBC Part 9

66.title: Water and Sewer — comply with Water and Sewer Services By-law and this By-law

67.title: Retaining Wall — ≤600mm not supporting structure: no permit required

68.title: Retaining Wall — >600mm and <1200mm not supporting structure: designed against sliding/overturning, drawings per Schedule E

69.title: Retaining Wall — >1200mm or supporting structure: RDP-designed with letters of assurance

70.title: Signs — designed per NBC Part 4 unless otherwise permitted by Inspector

71.title: Repeals — By-law No. 4469 and amendments repealed

A.title: Offences and Penalties (Voluntary Fines) — $200/$1000 for failure to comply; $50 fail to display; $500 fail to comply with permit conditions

B.title: Concrete Piers — Case 1 (8'), Case 2 (10'), Case 3 (15'), Case 4 (20') with rebar / tie / embed / lap / concrete specs + drawings (pp. 29-32)

C.title: Energy NBC Part 9 Residential — ANSI/ASHRAE 140 energy modelling; airtightness 3.0/1.5 ACH50 mid-construction/final; EnerGuide label required; performance compliance per Art. 2.2.8.3 Div C NBC

D.title: Energy NBC Part 3 Residential — ASHRAE 62.1-2001 ventilation; Prescriptive Table 3 RSI values (W:6.82/BG:5.95/Ceil:9.09/SOG:3.30/Exp:8.75) + FDWR 17%; Performance Path 1.50 L/(s·m²) @ 75Pa, TEDI 120 / TEUI 225 kWh/m²a; NBC 5.2.10 heat recovery; NBC 5.2.12 mechanical efficiency

E.title: Retaining Walls — Reinforced Concrete (≤1200mm, 25MPa/40mm cover), P.T. 6x6 Timber, Allan Block design summaries with drawings (pp. 37-39)

§64 Table 1 (NBC Part 9 Prescriptive).description: Effective RSI/R: W 5.28/30, Walls BG 4.96/28, Attic 10.6/60, Cathedral 7.0/40, SOG 5.64/32, Exp 7.0/40, Floor above unheated 6.28/36, Insulation skirt 5.64/32, Doors 0.91/0.16, Windows 1.00/0.18, FDWR 15%

§64 Table 2 (NBC Part 9 Performance).description: TEDI target 105 kWh/(m²a), %<Ref (no 9.36.5 or ERS base loads) -25%

Schedule D Table 3 (NBC Part 3 Residenti.description: Effective RSI/R: Walls AG 6.82/38, BG 5.95/34, Ceil 9.09/52, SOG 3.30/18, Exp 8.75/50, Floor above unheated 8.55/49, Perimeter 3.30/18, Doors 1.4/0.25, Windows 1.4/0.25, FDWR 17%

Schedule D Table 4 (NBC Part 3 Residenti.description: TEDI 120 kWh/(m²a), TEUI 225 kWh/(m²a), air leakage 1.50 L/(s·m²) @ 75 Pa
